hero.title: 4 Days in Kerala.
hero.titleAccent: Backwaters, Tea Hills and a Houseboat Night.
hero.intro: Kerala earned its nickname. In four days you slide from the colonial charm of Kochi to a night drifting through the Alleppey backwaters on your own houseboat, with palm-fringed canals and village life floating past.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days Explore Kerala: God's Own Country

overview.heading: 4 Days Explore Kerala: God's Own Country: The Full Picture
body: Kerala, tucked along India's lush southwest coast, is one of the most relaxing destinations in the country. Backwaters threaded with coconut palms, beaches, spice gardens, and a gentle pace that soothes from the moment you arrive. This is slow travel at its loveliest.

This 4 day Kerala package from Malaysia balances the heritage of Fort Kochi with the highlight every visitor remembers: a night aboard a traditional houseboat on the Alleppey backwaters. Your private vehicle and driver guide link it all together, with meals served onboard as you cruise.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Overnight Houseboat on the Backwaters
items.body: A converted rice barge becomes your floating hotel for a night on the Alleppey backwaters. Watch village life drift by, eat freshly cooked Kerala food onboard, and fall asleep to the sound of water.

items.title: Fort Kochi's Colonial Charm
items.body: The old harbour quarter, with its Chinese fishing nets, Dutch and Portuguese heritage, spice markets and art cafes, is a wonderful, walkable start to any Kerala trip.

items.title: Spice Gardens & Kerala Cuisine
items.body: Kerala's food is a revelation, from fragrant fish curries to the vegetarian sadya feast served on a banana leaf. The spice trade that shaped the region is still alive in its gardens and markets.

days.title: Arrive Kochi
days.description: Met at Cochin International Airport and transferred to your Fort Kochi hotel. Evening at the Chinese fishing nets.

days.title: Kochi to Alleppey Houseboat
days.description: Morning Kochi sightseeing, then drive to Alleppey to board your private houseboat for an overnight backwater cruise.

days.title: Backwaters to Kochi
days.description: A peaceful morning cruise, then disembark and return towards Kochi, with time for a spice or craft stop.

days.title: Depart Kochi
days.description: Checkout and transfer to the airport for your onward flight.

hotelOptions.heading: Choose Your Comfort Level
hotelOptions.subheading: Centrally located hotels with easy access to the main sights. Pick the tier that suits your style.

whyBook.heading: Kerala, Unhurried and Done Right
whyBook.body: The magic of Kerala is in the pacing, and a good houseboat and the right route make all the difference. We arrange quality houseboats and link everything with a private vehicle. Dhesu has sent Malaysians to Kerala since 1988.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We guide you through India e-Visa documentation with every booking.

points.title: Private Tours, Daily Departures
points.body: No minimum group to wait for. Your trip runs on your dates, your pace, your own vehicle and guide.

points.title: A Real Team to Call
points.body: Reachable throughout your trip. Flight delayed, your guide waits. Plans change, we adapt on the ground.

points.title: Group & Custom Pricing
points.body: Travelling as a family or group lowers the per-person cost. Ask us for a tailored quote.

sections.heading: Kerala Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 4 day Kerala trip is the perfect short escape into God's Own Country. It combines the heritage of Fort Kochi with the headline experience of an overnight houseboat on the Alleppey backwaters.

Going private means a relaxed pace and a houseboat to yourselves, with meals cooked onboard as you drift through the canals.

Want more time in the tea hills and wildlife of Munnar and Thekkady? See our 5, 6 and 8 day Kerala packages.

sections.heading: The Alleppey Backwater Experience
sections.body:
The Kerala backwaters are a network of canals, lagoons and lakes fringed by coconut palms and paddy fields. An overnight houseboat, a converted kettuvallam rice barge, is the classic way to experience them.

You cruise past villages, fishermen and lush greenery, with a crew cooking fresh Kerala meals onboard. It is one of the most relaxing nights you can spend anywhere in India.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Cochin International Airport (COK). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What is the best time to visit Kerala?
items.answer: September to March is ideal, with pleasant weather and beautiful greenery after the monsoon. The monsoon (June to August) is lush and atmospheric but wet. Kerala is warm and humid year-round given its tropical coastal setting.

items.question: Is the houseboat private?
items.answer: Yes. This package includes a private houseboat for your group, with a dedicated crew who cook fresh Kerala meals onboard. You cruise the Alleppey backwaters and spend the night aboard.

items.question: Can I extend to Munnar and Thekkady?
items.answer: Yes. Munnar's tea hills and Thekkady's spice plantations and wildlife are popular additions. Our 5, 6 and 8 day Kerala packages include these alongside Kochi and the backwaters.
